# wyred-audit test fixtures — goldens + tamper-mutated goldens (F6)

Generated by `python3 tests/make_fixtures.py` from
`wyred-contract/goldens/ga019` (set `WYRED_CONTRACT_GOLDENS` to point
elsewhere). Run the acceptance with `python3 tests/run_fixture_tests.py`.

## pristine/

Untouched byte-copies of five golden artifact sets, chosen to cover every
record kind the audit verifies:

| set | why |
|---|---|
| `watchy_v1_reva` | locked emit: retained baseline + connlock record; fork PARENT of revb |
| `watchy_v1_revb` | series fork: baseline + connlock + **lifecycle** record |
| `intent_05a_pinned` | locked emit with a baseline but NO connector freeze (baseline-only path) |
| `watchy_v1_draft` | the ECO INCUMBENT: its pinmap+alloc are the re-derivation inputs for btn3's pinmapdiff |
| `watchy_v1_draft_btn3` | incumbent-seeded ECO re-solve: the one golden **pinmapdiff** record |

The audit must exit 0 on this tree (rebuild honesty is skipped — the tree
is deliberately partial; the full-corpus rebuild is exercised against a
fresh `wyred.emit` output instead).

## tampered/<case>/

Each case is a full copy of `pristine/` with **exactly one** mutation
(diff any case against `pristine/` to see it). The audit must exit 1 and
fire the named code. Formatting is preserved (`json.dumps(indent=2,
sort_keys=True) + "\n"`, the engine's own byte form), so only the mutated
field differs.

| case | mutated file | mutation | must fire |
|---|---|---|---|
| `locked_row_edit` | `watchy_v1_reva.l1.json` | first `locked_by`-carrying allocation entry's `unit` bumped +1 — a locked decision edited in place, no `break_lock`, no fork | `LOCK_VIOLATION` (retained-baseline gate; `BASELINE_DRIFT` also fires — the re-derived snapshot no longer matches the retained one) |
| `series_hand_edit` | `watchy_v1_reva.l1.json` | `"series": "A"` → `"AZ"` with NO `forked_from` record — hand-editing the series string to "legalize" locked edits | `SERIES_UNJUSTIFIED` (plus legitimate cascades: reva is revb's fork parent, so revb's recorded lifecycle re-derivation now disagrees too) |
| `lifecycle_ok_flip` | `watchy_v1_revb.lifecycle.json` | engine-written `"ok": true` → `false` — forging the self-certified verdict field F3 warned about | `LIFECYCLE_DISAGREE` (harness re-derivation says `true`) |
| `connlock_ok_flip` | `watchy_v1_reva.connlock.json` | engine-written `"ok": true` → `false` | `CONNLOCK_DISAGREE` (audit re-derivation says `true`) |
| `bom_value_swap` | `watchy_v1_reva.bom.json` | the `value` payloads of two BOM line items swapped — sets and counts stay legal, the part-to-buy payload changes | `CROSSCHECK_FAILED` echoing the engine differential's `XPATH_BOM_FIELDS`; `REBUILD_FROM_PRIMARIES` (the bytes no longer rebuild from l2+alloc) |
| `pinmapdiff_blanked` | `watchy_v1_draft_btn3.pinmapdiff.json` | the ECO diff's `allocation.changed` + `terminals` lists blanked — the engine-lobotomy shape R1 warned about (a differ/incumbency check that reports nothing stays green) | `PINMAPDIFF_DISAGREE` (the audit's independent re-derivation from the two pinmap+alloc pairs disagrees) |
| `sticky_alloc_moved` | `watchy_v1_draft_btn3.alloc.json` | a SOLVER-chosen sticky allocation moved to a free unit while the pinmapdiff keeps claiming `minimal_disturbance_violations: []` | `PINMAPDIFF_DISAGREE` + `MINIMAL_DISTURBANCE_NOT_OK` (re-derived `_check_incumbency` finds the disturbance; `REBUILD_FROM_PRIMARIES` also fires — pinmap/records no longer rebuild from the tampered alloc) |
| `bom_totals_edit` | `watchy_v1_reva.bom.json` | `authored_total`/`generated_total` shifted by one — a payload field NO semantic gate reads | `REBUILD_FROM_PRIMARIES` **only** (the runner asserts `CROSSCHECK_FAILED` does NOT fire: the byte-level from-primaries rebuild catches what the semantic differential ignores) |

Regenerating: `make_fixtures.py` deletes and rebuilds `pristine/` and
`tampered/` wholesale; never hand-edit fixture files.
